examples.title: Nearest Centroid Classifier Implementation
examples.description: Complete implementation of NCC with training, prediction, and shrinkage regularization
examples.explanation: This implementation computes class centroids during fit() and predicts by finding the nearest centroid using Euclidean distance. The shrinkage parameter allows regularization to prevent overfitting.
examples.timeComplexity: Training: O(n×d), Prediction: O(k×d) per sample
examples.spaceComplexity: O(k×d) - stores only centroids, not training data

examples.title: Decision Boundary Computation
examples.description: Derive the linear decision boundary equation between two class centroids
examples.code:
import numpy as np

def compute_decision_boundary(c1, c2):
 """
 Compute the linear decision boundary between two centroids.
 The boundary is the perpendicular bisector of c1-c2.
 """
 # Midpoint
 midpoint = (c1 + c2) / 2
 
 # Normal vector (direction from c1 to c2)
 normal = c2 - c1
 
 # Decision boundary: normal · (x - midpoint) = 0
 # This expands to: normal · x = normal · midpoint
 
 print(f"Centroid 1: {c1}")
 print(f"Centroid 2: {c2}")
 print(f"Midpoint: {midpoint}")
 print(f"Normal vector: {normal}")
 print(f"Decision boundary: {normal[0]}x₁ + {normal[1]}x₂ = {np.dot(normal, midpoint)}")
 
 return midpoint, normal

# Example: c1 = [2,3], c2 = [8,7]
c1 = np.array([2, 3])
c2 = np.array([8, 7])
m, n = compute_decision_boundary(c1, c2)
examples.explanation: This code computes the equation of the linear decision boundary. The boundary is perpendicular to the line connecting centroids and passes through the midpoint. For 2D data, this gives a line equation.

examples.title: NCC with Cosine Similarity
examples.description: NCC variant using cosine similarity instead of Euclidean distance for high-dimensional/text data
examples.explanation: Cosine similarity measures the angle between vectors rather than Euclidean distance. This works better for high-dimensional data like text embeddings where magnitude differences are less important than directional alignment.
